commands.summary: Built the ForgeLink human MCP bridge and verified tools, resources, prompts, templates, and approval/action behavior.

Started a temporary ForgeLink backend, created an MCP token file, loaded forgelink-human through stdio, emitted a Fabric-style mcp_manifest, mapped capability rows, created a request_human_approval message, recorded an approve action, and observed the acted outcome through MCP.
Full Electron/backend/renderer regression suite passed after the Fabric integration smoke tooling changes.

environment.fabric_manifest_shape: Matches ForgeWire Fabric runner mcp_manifest schema_version 1 with servers[*].tools/resources/prompts.
environment.runtime_mismatch: No Fabric runtime mismatch found for the integration boundary. Live Fabric hub registration remains external to ForgeLink; this item uses a high-fidelity local smoke that mirrors Fabric's manifest and capability-index shape without adding Python to ForgeLink.
environment.redaction_note: The smoke artifact omits MCP token values and approval body text while retaining tool names, resource URIs, prompt names, message id, channel, source, kind, urgency, and action outcome.
